name: disjoint_l2_commented_out_intra_table
code:
proof fn phi_4_disjoint_l2_intra_table_missing(pt: PageTable, p: PageMapPtr, l2i: L2Index, l2j: L2Index)
    requires
        pt.wf_l2(),
        pt.disjoint_l2(),
        pt.l2_tables@.dom().contains(p),
        0 <= l2i < 512,
        0 <= l2j < 512,
        l2i != l2j,
        pt.l2_tables@[p].value()[l2i].perm.present,
        pt.l2_tables@[p].value()[l2j].perm.present,
        !pt.l2_tables@[p].value()[l2i].perm.ps,
        !pt.l2_tables@[p].value()[l2j].perm.ps,
    ensures
        pt.l2_tables@[p].value()[l2i].addr != pt.l2_tables@[p].value()[l2j].addr,
{
}
reason: disjoint_l2 covers intra-table uniqueness via the pi==pj && l2i!=l2j case — verifying this confirms the active (non-commented) spec correctly replaced the commented-out version

{

reason: pcid_ioid_wf uses `!=` (XOR) meaning exactly one must be Some — a page table can never have both PCID and IOID simultaneously, but the spec allows neither to be set (both None satisfies != trivially as false != false is false... actually both None gives false != false = false, so this is not satisfied — the XOR is correct)
